calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300042 The Estate of David Carl Robinson and
PES-16-300043 The Estate of Rosa Lee Robinson.

Appearances: Natalie Lupo for Carolyn Robinson.

The Court asks if Ms. Lupo has had discussion with any Professional Fiduciary. Ms. Lupo stated that she guesses she can speak with someone and that she believes Ronald lives in the Wallace Street property. The Court granted the Motions to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to review of the order). The Court will determine what action to take with the remaining matters.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account And The Amended Probate Orders
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Continued To Apr-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300042 Estate of David Carl Robinson and PES-16-300043 Estate of Rosa Lee Robinson.

Appearances: Natalie Lupo for Carolyn Robinson (suspended Administrator).

The Court stated that it was hoping some of the other Beneficiaries would be here. Ms. Lupo stated that she reached out to Carolyn, that Carolyn has not communicated back, that there is a breakdown in communication, and that she sent letters to the Beneficiaries and to the Bank to advise them that Ms. Robinson has been suspended. Ms. Lupo also stated that someone from the Public Administrator's Office was here in January, that the Public Administrator felt it was not appropriate for them to step in, that the last time she heard from the Administrator she indicated that they did not want to do anything with the Wallace Street property and that she is not sure if Carolyn would qualify for a bond. All matters are continued to April 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Court to figure out what to do.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account And The Amended Probate Orders
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300042 Estate of David Carl Robinson and
PES-16-300043 Estate of Rosa Lee Robinson.

Appearances: Natalie Lupo for Carolyn Robinson (Administrator).

Ms. Lupo stated that she served notice of the motion, that she sent a letter to the Beneficiaries informing them that the Administrator has been suspended, that she also sent a letter Bank of America and that it was done within one week from the last hearing. Ms. Lupo also stated that she has not heard anything from the Beneficiaries or the Administrator, that the Administrator is in San Francisco, but she heard that the Administrator is moving, that $55,406.00 was the last amount of funds they heard from the Client, that it was to be placed in a blocked account, that there is a property in San Francisco located at 1635 Wallace Street, that there is a deed of trust on it and that her Client said she no longer wanted to retain their services. In addition, Ms. Lupo stated that she filed a copy of the letter she sent to the bank, but not the one she sent to the Beneficiaries. The Court expressed concern about relieving Ms. Lupo and nothing being done to close out the estate. The Court also directed Ms. Lupo to file a copy of the letter she sent to the Beneficiaries. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Beneficiaries to appear on April 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Motions to Be Relieved as Counsel, status on filing of the receipts for the blocked account, status reports of administration and the orders to show cause are continued to April 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account And The Amended Probate Orders
Off Calendar Jan-14-2019 Continued To Mar-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300042 Estate of David Carl Robinson and
PES-16-300043 Estate of Rosa Lee Robinson.

Appearances: Natalie Lupo for Carolyn Robinson.
Janet Boessenecker for Public Administrator.

Ms. Lupo stated that she filed Motions to Be Relieved as Counsel on December 21st and January 4th, that she tried contacting her Client on December 21st, and that at the time of their last communication Ms. Robinson indicated that she no longer wished to retain their services and that she is going to do this on her own. Ms. Boessenecker stated that she reviewed the files, that when the Mother passed away there were three children, that one child still lives in the home, that the third child post deceased and his estate is being probated and that they now have the Daughter. Ms. Boessenecker also stated that there is a $200,000.00 plus lien on the property, that if the Public Administrator comes in they will need to sell the property and the party living in the house will be displaced, that her Client's practice is to protect the elderly and the disabled, that this party may have been one of their Clients and that her Client does not feel this is an appropriate case for them to act. In addition, Ms. Boessenecker stated that there seems to be $800,000.00 in equity, that maybe a Private Fiduciary would be better to act and that she does not feel the Public Administrator can step in at this point. Ms. Lupo stated that she has not spoken with any of the other Beneficiaries and that she is not aware if they are represented by Counsel. The Court will remove Carolyn Robinson as the Administrator of the estate. Counsel needs to send a letter to the bank to inform them that the letters of administration are revoked, that the Administrator is removed and that no one has authority to act (a copy of said letter is to be filed). Ms. Lupo also needs to send a letter to Carolyn Robinson with copies to all of the Beneficiaries advising them that no one is administering the estate, that they will not have access to funds, that they will have no authority and that they will need to file there own petition to have someone appointed. All matters are continued to March 11, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The hearing date of January 29, 2019 is also continued to March 11, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved Of Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Aug-27-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300042 The Estate of David Carl Robinson and
PES-16-300043 The Estate of Rosa Lee Robinson.

Appearances: Neil Rones for and with Administrator.
Sheila Robello making a special appearance as a potential Successor Attorney.

Ms. Robello stated that she has agreed to be the new Attorney, that she is here to see what happens and that if the Court is ready to proceed, she will come in. Mr. Rones stated that as soon as Ms. Robinson gets a tax identification number she will go to Bank of America and open a blocked account. Ms. Robinson stated that there is about $70,000.00 left from the loan proceeds. Ms. Robello stated that she believes the loan amount was about $200,000.00, that the amount taken out is less than Ms. Robinson's inheritance and that there will be enough to cover the loan. Mr. Rones informed the Court that the loan is due in January of 2020. The Court raised the issue of Ms. Robinson obtaining a bond. Ms. Robello raised the possibility of Ms. Robinson having limited authority and stated that there might be an issue with Ms. Robinson qualifying for a $900,000.00 bond. Ms. Robello also stated that she will submit an amended order granting limited authority, file an ex parte petition for a blocked account, do a substitution of attorney and asked for a little bit of time for the accounting. The Motions to Be Relieved As Counsel is off calendar. The Petitions for Approval of Report on Status of Administration are continued to November 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account and the amended probate orders is set for September 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved Of Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Continued To Aug-27-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300042 The Estate of David Carl Robinson and
PES-16-300043 The Estate of Rosa Lee Robinson.

Appearances: Neil Rones for and with Carolyn Robinson.

Ms. Robinson apologized to the Court for not appearing at the last hearing, stated that she had purchased a ticket to go out of town, that when she reached out to the Probate Examiner she was told that there was nothing he could do for her because she has an Attorney, and that she was under the impression that Mr. Rone was no longer her Attorney. Ms. Robinson also stated that she does not have a new Attorney, that she has tried to hire someone, that every time the new Attorney tries to get a hold of Mr. Rone he won't contact them, and that she is trying to get a new Attorney, but they will not take her case because they can't get any information from Mr. Rone. Mr. Rone stated that he has not heard from anyone, that he ran a title search and found that Ms. Robinson acting as the Administrator borrowed $200,000.00 from some private lenders, that he never heard from these people, that they did not file a notice of proposed action and that he does not know what the funds were used for. Mr. Rone also stated that there is a deed of Trust now filed against the property, that he has a copy of the deed, but he did not file it, that he is concerned about Ms. Robinson's actions and that David's Child had notice of the petition, but not of today's hearing date. Ms. Robinson stated that David's Daughter is Shaland Robinson, that she is about 42 years of age, that Shaland evidently is not interested and that she never came forward. Furthermore, Ms. Robinson stated that she borrowed the money to get out of debt, to pay Mr. Rone and to find a new Attorney, that Miguel Angel helped her with the loan and reached out to an Attorney that was willing to help her, but this Attorney was unable to reach Mr. Rone, and that she only got $100,000.00 after paying an amount owed on the house and Mr. Angel's fees. The Court informed Ms. Robinson that Mr. Rone is still her Attorney, that it will not approve his removal until she has a new Attorney and that she has to hire a new Attorney. The Court also suggested Ms. Robinson bring Mr. Angel to Court, informed her that she can be suspended and stated that it may consider bringing in the Public Administrator. The Motions to be Relieved As Counsel and the Petitions for Approval of Report on Status of Administration are continued to August 27, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
